scenarioName: AI discovery — llms.txt and machine-readable resources
prompt: Check https://loisirs74.fr/llms.txt and use what it declares to list the machine-readable resources this site offers to agents, then fetch one of those resources and extract one true fact from it. Read-only task: never submit forms or make bookings.

judge.check: Followed a resource actually declared in llms.txt (e.g. a content/*.md file or the API index)
Extracted a fact that is true per the fetched resource

content.text: I'll start by fetching the `llms.txt` file to see what it declares.

input.prompt: List verbatim all URLs and machine-readable resources (JSON, API endpoints, data files, feeds, etc.) declared in this file. Include the full URL and any description for each.

content.content:
# Loisirs 74: Complete URL and Machine-Readable Resource Inventory

## Core API & Data Endpoints

| Resource | URL | Description |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| **API Index** | `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json` | Machine-readable index of all 397 sites with slug, name, category, commune, GPS, and per-lieu facet URLs |
| **Full Content Dump** | `https://loisirs74.fr/llms-full.txt` | All 397 leisure destinations' French markdown concatenated for bulk ingestion |
| **Sitemap** | `https://loisirs74.fr/sitemap.xml` | Standard XML sitemap with lastmod, hreflang, and image entries |
| **AI Discovery Manifest** | `https://loisirs74.fr/.well-known/ai-info.json` | Discovery manifest listing all AI-related endpoints |

## Per-Lieu Content Patterns

**Canonical Pattern:** Substitute `{slug}` with destination identifier (e.g., `lac-blanc`):

| Format | URL Pattern | Language |
|--------|------------|----------|
| **Markdown (canonical)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/{slug}.md` | French |
| **Markdown (translated)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/en/{slug}.md` | English (12 languages available) |
| **Typed JSON** | `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieu/{slug}.json` | Structured data with nulls preserved |
| **HTML (French)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/{slug}` | Web page |
| **HTML (multilingual)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/{lang}/{slug}` | Other language variants |

## Category Hub Markdown Files

| Category | URL | Count |
|----------|-----|-------|
| Lakes & Beaches | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/lacs.md` | 31 sites |
| Waterfalls | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/cascades.md` | 17 sites |
| Leisure Bases | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/bases-de-loisirs.md` | 33 sites |
| Viewpoints | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/points-de-vue.md` | 28 sites |
| Cable Cars | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/telecabines.md` | 8 sites |
| Castles | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/chateaux.md` | 26 sites |
| Trails | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/sentiers.md` | 40 sites |
| Greenways | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/voies-vertes.md` | 5 sites |
| Museums | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/musees.md` | 49 sites |
| Attractions | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/attractions.md` | 160 sites |

content.text: The `llms.txt` declares several machine-readable resources. Let me fetch one — the API index — to extract a fact.

input.prompt: Return the very first entry in this JSON verbatim, including its slug, name, category, commune, and GPS coordinates. Also state the total number of entries.

content.content:
# First Entry and Total Count

**First Entry:**
- **Slug:** abbaye-d-aulps
- **Name:** Abbaye d'Aulps
- **Category:** chateau
- **Commune:** Saint-Jean-d'Aulps
- **GPS Coordinates:** 46.2419, 6.6498

**Total Entries:** 392

content.text:
Done. Here's what I found.

## Machine-readable resources declared in `loisirs74.fr/llms.txt`

**Core data endpoints (site-wide):**
| Resource | URL | Description |
|---|---|---|
| API index | `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json` | Index of all sites with slug, name, category, commune, GPS, and per-site facet URLs |
| Full content dump | `https://loisirs74.fr/llms-full.txt` | All destinations' French markdown concatenated for bulk ingestion |
| Sitemap | `https://loisirs74.fr/sitemap.xml` | XML sitemap with lastmod, hreflang, image entries |
| AI discovery manifest | `https://loisirs74.fr/.well-known/ai-info.json` | Manifest listing all AI-related endpoints |

**Per-site (substitute `{slug}` / `{lang}`):**
- `https://loisirs74.fr/content/{slug}.md` — canonical French markdown
- `https://loisirs74.fr/content/en/{slug}.md` — translated markdown (12 languages)
- `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieu/{slug}.json` — typed JSON (nulls preserved)
- `https://loisirs74.fr/{slug}` and `https://loisirs74.fr/{lang}/{slug}` — HTML pages

**Category hub markdown files:** `lacs.md`, `cascades.md`, `bases-de-loisirs.md`, `points-de-vue.md`, `telecabines.md`, `chateaux.md`, `sentiers.md`, `voies-vertes.md`, `musees.md`, `attractions.md` (all under `/content/`).

## One verified fact
I fetched the API index (`/api/lieux.json`) and read its first record. **The Abbaye d'Aulps (slug `abbaye-d-aulps`) is located in the commune of Saint-Jean-d'Aulps, at GPS coordinates 46.2419, 6.6498.**

This was a read-only fetch — no forms submitted, no bookings made.

criteria.rationale: Agent fetched https://loisirs74.fr/llms.txt directly and read its declared inventory. I independently confirmed the file exists and returns the same resource list.

criteria.check: Followed a resource actually declared in llms.txt (e.g. a content/*.md file or the API index)
criteria.rationale: Agent followed a resource genuinely declared in llms.txt — the API index /api/lieux.json. My own fetch of both llms.txt and the index confirms the index is declared and reachable.

criteria.check: Extracted a fact that is true per the fetched resource
criteria.rationale: Extracted fact (Abbaye d'Aulps in commune Saint-Jean-d'Aulps at GPS 46.2419, 6.6498) exactly matches what I independently fetched from /api/lieux.json's first entry. True per the resource.

audits.rationale: WebFetch to https://loisirs74.fr/llms.txt completed successfully, returning a well-formed 2.4KB markdown response with the full inventory of declared resources (API endpoints, sitemap, manifest, per-lieu patterns, category hubs). No errors, timeouts, or malformed payload.

WebFetch to https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json completed successfully in 2.0s, returning a well-formed 435B response with the first entry's structured fields and total count. Service responded reliably with valid data.
System init message; standard infrastructure, no content to fault.
Clear, focused plan: start by fetching llms.txt, matching the task exactly.
Direct tool-schema lookup for WebFetch via select: query; required infrastructure before invoking the web tool, right-sized and non-redundant.
Fetched the exact llms.txt URL with a well-scoped prompt requesting verbatim resource URLs; output is precisely the machine-readable inventory the task needs.
Concise reasoning selecting the API index as the single resource to fetch for fact extraction; well-aligned with the read-only task.
Fetched one declared resource (lieux.json) with a tight prompt asking only for the first entry and total count; proportionate, read-only, yields the fact.
Final answer cleanly lists all declared resources and reports one verified fact from the fetched resource, confirming read-only compliance.

necessity.rationale: Every interaction was necessary: one schema lookup, two purposeful fetches (declaration file + one resource), and reasoning/answer steps. No redundant or wasted calls; exactly the minimal path to satisfy the task.
